mechanism: A single Entra ID app registration serves as the platform identity. Created during initialization before any Azure infrastructure exists. Authenticates to GitHub via OIDC and holds permissions to provision landing zone identities.
why: Managed identities are bound to Azure resources and can't exist before one does. The platform identity must exist before any subscription. App registrations are pure Entra ID objects — they satisfy the bootstrap constraint and receive Graph application permissions through standard admin consent.

related.note: Must carry a federated credential scoped to platform workflows.
Graph permissions let it assign Directory Reader to landing zone identities.
Platform identity provisions each landing zone's managed identity and permissions.
Entra app is Azure-side; GitHub App is GitHub-side — together they bootstrap both.

violations: Managed identity used as platform identity — can't exist before Azure infrastructure.
Multiple app registrations for different platform workflows — fragmented permissions.
Platform app registration shared with non-platform workloads.
